name: wizard
description: A multi-step form stepper (CONTROLLED headless PARTIAL). The active step is a server fact owned by the CALLER's @Wire int field; this partial renders the presentational chrome only: an ordered step-indicator strip (linear or skippable), a content panel (the active step's body via a Content slot), and a Prev/Next/Submit action bar. Skippable mode composes collection-nav.enhancer.ts (APG roving-tabindex, Arrow/Home/End/Enter navigation on step buttons). A11y: WAI-ARIA aria-current=step + W3C WAI Multi-page Forms tutorial (ordered-list + aria-current model, NOT role=tablist). Features: linear/skippable modes, horizontal/vertical orientation, bottom/inline label placement, number/icon/dot indicator variants, sm/md/lg sizes, per-step status (process/finish/error/wait), visually-hidden SR status text, aria-live progress announcement. Pure HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens; no Java component, no @Wire, no _component/_instance params; CSP-safe.
docs: Copies wizard.jte to your JTE template root at lievit/wizard.jte. Reach as @template.lievit.wizard(activeStep=myForm.activeStep(), stepIds=..., stepTitles=..., content=@`...`). The CALLER's @Wire component owns int activeStep and exposes next()/prev()/goTo(int) actions; the partial wires l:click to them via the prevAction/nextAction/goToAction params (default: 'prev'/'next'/'goTo'). Skippable mode requires collection-nav.enhancer.ts installed (installCollectionNav(runtime)) and allowedSteps set to the reachable step indexes. The content slot renders the active step's owned markup; use @if(myForm.activeStep() == N) blocks inside to swap per-step content. The sub-partials wizard/step.jte and wizard/footer.jte from the previous API are removed; all chrome is self-contained in this single template.
